title: The Ageing House
content:
When the walls were red
     That now are seen
     To be overspread
     With a mouldy green,
     A fresh fair head
     Would often lean
     From the sunny casement
     And scan the scene,
    While blithely spoke the wind to the little sycamore tree.

     But storms have raged
     Those walls about,
     And the head has aged
     That once looked out;
     And zest is suaged
     And trust is doubt,
     And slow effacement
     Is rife throughout,
    While fiercely girds the wind at the long-limbed sycamore tree!
meaning:
This evocative piece by Thomas Hardy, titled "The Ageing House", represents a masterful exploration of classic. The lines capture a profound emotional resonance...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
